sourceMovie.title: Seven Swords
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Seven Swords (2005) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Gladiator II
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Seven Swords clicked for you, Gladiator II (2024) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Seven Swords?
faq.answer: Try Gladiator II, Ip Man, House of Flying Daggers—each shares DNA with Seven Swords while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Seven Swords?
faq.answer: Seven Swords (2005) is best described as Action, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Seven Swords worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
